page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9T24 processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused allocation of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is anchored by a 16 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top tier for raw computational power. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both rank in the top 25%, supported by strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and high PassMark CPU scores. Cryptographic capabilities are enhanced by hardware-accelerated instruction sets including AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, which is reflected in the strong PassMark CPU encryption benchmark. Memory performance is highly efficient for cached operations, registering a strong read-only small block bandwidth of 3646.1 GB/sec, though uncached large block read and write speeds fall into the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated core allocation provides predictable compute performance without virtualization overhead or thread contention. The lack of integrated local storage requires reliance on external block storage, which represents a structural tradeoff for disk-heavy workloads. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound applications that do not require massive memory footprints, such as high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, cryptographic processing, and Redis-based in-memory database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T24 processor at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, this KVM-based server provides a 1:2 core-to-memory ratio designed for high-density computational workloads. It lacks local storage but delivers a 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates strong performance, with stress-ng CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations are accelerated via AES and SHA_NI instruction sets, while Redis SET operations reach 64.43 million operations per second. This instance is optimized for compute-heavy tasks, cryptographic processing, and high-throughput database operations where local storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.12xlarge features 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 96 GB RAM, delivering top-tier CPU and cryptographic performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance delivering strong multi-core processing and high-throughput cryptographic performance without local storage.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 9T24 cores running at 2.7 GHz.
96.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Top 25% performance in stress-ng CPU and PassMark benchmarks.
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage.
Hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.
Best-fit for multi-threaded decompression, Redis databases, and compute-heavy workloads.
